name: Timmie Rogers
abstract: Timmie Rogers (born Timothy Louis Ancrum July 4, 1915 – December 17, 2006) was an American comedian, singer-songwriter, bandleader and actor who appeared on many national TV shows in the 1960s and 1970s. Rogers was one of the first Black comedians allowed to directly address a white audience when he worked.

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Mingus
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Timmie Rogers when Erroll Garner's brother was on it.

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Tate
about_quotes.evidence: we came to New York with Mamie Smith... Timmie Rogers and Freddie was together. We played theaters with her.

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: I know a guy who would love you... Oh, his name was Rogers... Timmie Rogers was the guy.
